//order: convention. NOT a formatting grab-bag: date-format policy lives in the rubric
//form: and regex/sort FORM lives in C#. fontAllowlist enters here only in the change that
//font: gives rubric criterion D3 a reader — landed in #891. NO AI/LLM.
//fontAllowlist: The rubric's D3 atsPassSignal font list ('Arial, Calibri, Helvetica, Roboto,
//fontAllowlist2: Times New Roman, Georgia, Verdana') made machine-readable — a RECOMMENDATION,
//fontAllowlist3: parallel to sectionOrder. It is an EXEMPLAR set, not an exhaustive list of
//fontAllowlist4: ATS-safe fonts (Garamond/Lato parse fine and are absent), so D3 WARNS on a
//fontAllowlist5: non-member body font, never FAILS. The <10 pt band is a THRESHOLD and lives
//sectionOrder: The rubric's B1 atsPassSignal made machine-readable: 'Kontakt -> (Profil) ->
//sectionOrder2: Arbetslivserfarenhet -> Utbildning -> Kompetenser -> Sprak -> Ovrigt'. The ids are
//sectionOrder3: the section identities the lexicon and ParsedSectionKind already own — this asset
//sectionOrder4: mints none of its own. The trailing 'Ovrigt' is NOT a field: it is the stability
//sectionOrder5: property of the sort (unnamed sections follow, keeping their observed relative
//sectionOrder6: order), and an algorithm belongs in C#, not in a boolean nobody can vary.
